problem: summary.management_risks[].rationale (src/output/json/helpers.rs:277-280) formats performance/mobile scores via {:?} on an Option<f64>, producing a visible Rust Debug-format leak (e.g. "performance Some(84), mobile Some(77)") instead of clean numbers. Separately, the "average accessibility score" quoted in that same rationale string is computed by average_accessibility_score() (src/output/json/helpers.rs:450) using integer-truncating division (sum / len), which can disagree with summary.accessibility_score's own rounded value for the same nominal statistic.
evidence: Found by the report-critic skill's dry-run eval against reports/casoon-batch-en301549.json: management_risks[1].rationale literally read '...performance Some(84), mobile Some(77).' and separately quoted "84" as the average accessibility score while summary.accessibility_score = 85 (85 = round(); 84 = truncating integer division of the same underlying per-page scores) — the JSON PDF text independently computes and shows 85 correctly, so only the raw JSON field is currently affected.
expected: rationale strings must format Option<f64> values as plain numbers (e.g. via unwrap_or / map, not {:?}), and any two fields both claiming to be "the average accessibility score" must use the same rounding rule so they can never silently disagree.
counter_examples: A management_risks rationale for a dimension that has no optional score inputs at all (nothing to format) is unaffected and must not be flagged.
